prose_hint: A short, plain answer; he says less than he knows and watches more than he speaks.
Weighed, quiet wisdom — a warning or a steadying word out of long, hard experience.
He gives little of himself away, courteous but keeping his name and errand close.
A few careful questions of his own, turning the talk to what he needs to learn.
A rare, brief warmth — a half-smile, a word of plain respect for one who has earned it.
She turns the question aside without answering, as though the asking wearied her.
A dry, cutting remark — true, and a little cruel — that closes the matter.
She says the bleak thing plainly: the thing the other did not want spoken aloud.
Courtesy worn thin by ages — correct, distant, and clearly eager to be done.
For a breath something unguarded slips through, and is shut away again at once.
